Operation 'removeItemFromCollection' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Description lacks critical details about what input is expected (entry identifier format), what the response contains, and the side effect (collection membership change). Added specifics about authentication method, input requirements, response format, and clarified that removal only affects collection membership, not the torrent entry itself.

data.current_summary: Remove an item from a collection
data.current_description: Remove a torrent entry from an existing collection. Requires authentication.
data.description_suggestion: Removes a torrent entry from an existing collection by name. Requires authentication via uid and pass credentials. Expects the torrent entry identifier in the request body. Returns success confirmation or error if the collection or entry does not exist. Modifies the collection membership - the entry remains in the repository but is no longer associated with this collection.
data.reasoning: Description lacks critical details about what input is expected (entry identifier format), what the response contains, and the side effect (collection membership change). Added specifics about authentication method, input requirements, response format, and clarified that removal only affects collection membership, not the torrent entry itself.

message: Operation 'testApiAccess' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_output_details, unclear_purpose. Original summary is generic. Description mentions method flexibility but lacks clarity on what constitutes a successful response, what errors might be returned, and the practical use case. Improved summary to be more specific about validation purpose, and enhanced description with response details and use case context for better AI agent decision-making.

data.current_description: Test that your API key is valid and you have access rights. Can be used with GET or POST.
data.description_suggestion: Validates that the provided API credentials (uid and pass) are valid and the authenticated user has active access rights. Supports both GET and POST methods. Returns a success response if authentication is valid, or an error response if credentials are invalid or access is revoked. Useful for verifying authentication setup before making other API calls.
data.reasoning: Original summary is generic. Description mentions method flexibility but lacks clarity on what constitutes a successful response, what errors might be returned, and the practical use case. Improved summary to be more specific about validation purpose, and enhanced description with response details and use case context for better AI agent decision-making.

message: Operation 'testApiAccessPost' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_output_details, poor_ai_interpretability. Summary is unclear about the distinction from GET variant and lacks actionable context. Description is too brief and doesn't explain when to use POST over GET, what inputs are expected, or what the response contains. Enhanced both to clarify the POST-specific use case, explain input flexibility, and provide response details for better AI agent interpretability regarding method selection.

data.current_description: Test that your API key is valid and you have access rights using POST method.
data.summary_suggestion: Verify API authentication credentials via POST
data.description_suggestion: Validates that the provided API credentials (uid and pass) are valid and the authenticated user has active access rights using the POST method. Accepts credentials in the request body or as query parameters. Returns a success response if authentication is valid, or an error response if credentials are invalid or access is revoked. This POST variant is useful when credentials cannot be passed as query parameters.
data.reasoning: Summary is unclear about the distinction from GET variant and lacks actionable context. Description is too brief and doesn't explain when to use POST over GET, what inputs are expected, or what the response contains. Enhanced both to clarify the POST-specific use case, explain input flexibility, and provide response details for better AI agent interpretability regarding method selection.

message: Operation 'updateEntry' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description lacks specificity about what fields can be modified, how partial updates work, what is returned, and the side effects of modification. Added details about authentication methods, partial update support, return value, and clarification that metadata is updated without affecting torrent data for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_description: Modify an existing torrent entry. Requires authentication via API key.
data.description_suggestion: Updates an existing torrent entry with new metadata. Requires authentication via uid and pass (cookies or query parameters). Accepts partial updates via EntryInput schema - only provided fields will be modified. Returns the updated Entry object. Modifying an entry updates its metadata in the repository without affecting the underlying torrent data.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks specificity about what fields can be modified, how partial updates work, what is returned, and the side effects of modification. Added details about authentication methods, partial update support, return value, and clarification that metadata is updated without affecting torrent data for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_description: Upload a new torrent entry to Academic Torrents. Requires authentication via API key (uid and pass).
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new torrent entry in Academic Torrents repository. Requires authentication via uid and pass (cookies or query parameters). Accepts torrent metadata via EntryCreateInput schema. Returns the newly created Entry object with assigned info hash and metadata. The entry becomes immediately available in the repository for discovery and download via BitTorrent.
data.reasoning: Original description is missing information about what is returned and the side effects of creating an entry. Added details about the return value (Entry object with info hash), authentication method options, and clarification that the entry becomes available in the repository, helping AI agents understand the full impact of this operation.

message: Operation 'createCollection' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description is too vague about what a collection is, what inputs are needed, what is returned, and the side effects. data.current_description: Create a new collection. Requires authentication.
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new named collection to organize and group torrent entries. Requires authentication via uid and pass (cookies or query parameters). Accepts collection metadata via CollectionInput schema. Returns the newly created Collection object. Collections serve as curated groupings of related torrent entries and are immediately available for adding entries and sharing.
data.reasoning: Original description is too vague about what a collection is, what inputs are needed, what is returned, and the side effects. Added domain context explaining collections are for organizing entries, specified authentication methods, input schema reference, return value, and clarified that collections become immediately available for use.

data.current_description: Update an existing collection. Requires authentication.

Updating a collection modifies its metadata without affecting the entries it contains.

message: Operation 'deleteCollection' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. data.current_description: Delete an existing collection. Requires authentication.
data.description_suggestion: Permanently deletes an existing collection identified by name. Requires authentication via uid and pass (cookies or query parameters). Returns success confirmation. Deleting a collection removes it from the repository; contained torrent entries are not affected and remain available in the repository.
data.reasoning: Original description is missing information about what is returned and the side effects of deletion. Added details about collection identification by name, authentication methods, return value, and important clarification that deleting a collection doesn't delete the entries it contained, only the collection itself.

data.current_summary: Add an item to a collection
data.current_description: Add a torrent entry to an existing collection. Requires authentication.
